methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Grain Weevil is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ben Johnson that has raised $3.5M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes one public comparable and eight private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression modeling. Using total capital raised ($3.5M, approximated at $4M) as a base value proxy, a 2.5x multiplier typical of the Seed-to-next-stage transition yields an implied valuation of $9M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 11 same-sector companies exist in the dataset, insufficient overlap in stage, timing, and valuation tier prevented reliable peer triangulation (minimum three datapoints required, zero filtered matches obtained).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $7M (low) to $10M (high), with a median point of $9M. This range reflects the mechanics of stage-progression alone and carries material uncertainty given the absence of corroborating peer-based triangulation. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier to Grain Weevil's specific stage transition, market conditions in robotics since capital deployment, and burn rate relative to milestones achieved. Investors should treat this as a preliminary framework pending stronger peer comparables or detailed discounted cash flow inputs.
